title: Veterinary Sales Leader (CSR Coach + Producer)
description:
Copperfield Animal Clinic in Texas is hiring a remote Sales Leader to help more pets get the care they need. Your work will directly increase appointments, reduce missed care, and help clients follow through so problems are caught early.



Many pet owners delay care because life gets busy, prices feel confusing, or they do not understand urgency. When calls are not booked or follow ups do not happen, pets miss exams, vaccines, screenings, and dentals that prevent bigger problems later. In this role you will coach our onsite CSRs to convert more calls into appointments and you will personally follow up with lapsed clients and unbooked callers so more pets come in and stay on track.



You will lead a simple system that improves booking rate and show rate while keeping communication calm and helpful. You will listen to call recordings, tighten scripts, and teach objection versus smoke screen so CSRs respond without pressure. You will run weekly video coaching with the onsite team, answer their sales questions, and hold them to clear standards. You will also do your own outreach by phone and text to bring back clients who have not visited in 18 plus months and to recover callers who did not book. A key focus is helping clients visit twice per year by supporting an annual exam now and a dental six months later, then confirming those dental appointments so pets actually receive the care.



This is a remote role with a paid trial. You must have proven phone sales or appointment setting experience and coaching experience is strongly preferred. You must speak and write English very well, you must be organized and consistent with tracking, and you must close without being pushy. You must have reliable high speed internet for clear calls with no disconnects, you must provide a speed test screenshot showing download and upload speed, and you must confirm you have a backup internet option.



To apply, reply with:

1. short summary of your phone sales and coaching experience

2. How you find whether a customers objection is legitimate or a smoke screen with one example of each

3. your internet speed test screenshot and backup plan 



Finalists will complete a short role play and a call review test in the final interview.

title: Customer Service Representative
description:
About Us

Private Duty Staffing (PDS) is a Human Resources Staffing and solutions company based in Michigan.  We aid companies in managing their manpower needs, and we search for talents across the globe to find the perfect fit for our client’s workforce.



Job Description

This is a remote position.



Role Overview

We are looking for a Customer Service Representative (CSR) to serve as a key point of contact for our customers. In this role, you will handle inquiries, resolve concerns, and provide accurate information across multiple communication channels.

This position is ideal for individuals who are customer-focused, detail-oriented, and comfortable working remotely in a fast-paced environment while supporting U.S.-based clients.





Key Responsibilities

- Respond to customer inquiries via phone,
, chat, or ticketing systems

- Provide clear, accurate, and professional support regarding services and processes

- Resolve customer concerns with empathy and a solutions-focused approach

- Document customer interactions accurately in internal systems

- Escalate complex issues to the appropriate team or supervisor when needed





Eligibility Requirements (Required)?

To be considered for this role, applicants must meet at least one of the following criteria:

- Have lived or worked in the United States, OR

- Have physically worked in the United States, OR

- Are currently or were previously a participant in the InterExchange Work Program

Applicants who meet at least one of the eligibility criteria above are encouraged to apply.





Requirements

- Previous experience in customer service, call center, or client support roles preferred

- Strong spoken and written English communication skills

- Excellent problem-solving, listening, and interpersonal skills

- Ability to multitask and manage time effectively in a remote setup

- Availability to work assigned schedules aligned with U.S. Eastern Standard Time (EST) business hours when required.



Preferred Skills

- Experience supporting U.S.-based customers

- Familiarity with CRM or ticketing systems

- Ability to remain calm and professional under pressure

- Reliable internet connection and professional home workspace





Benefits

- Competitive compensation based on experience and location

- Paid training and onboarding

- Performance-based incentives (where applicable)

- Opportunity for long-term engagement and growth



Why Join Us?

- Work remotely with an international team

- Clear expectations and supportive management

- Stable workload with structured processes

- Opportunity to grow your customer service career

title: Data Analyst (Operations Experienced)
description:
POSITION SUMMARY

The Data Analyst plays a key role in transforming raw data into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making across the organization. This position is responsible for building and maintaining Lookr dashboards, creating and automation reliable data sources, and overseeing the company’s daily, weekly, and monthly reporting infrastructure.



ABOUT CARDONE VENTURES

Our mission is to help business owners achieve their personal, professional, and financial goals through the growth of their businesses. We work in dozens of verticals and provide strategic business guidance through courses, live events, partnerships, and investments. Our core values are the backbone of our business and guide our hiring process: we are inspirational, disciplined, accountable, transparent, aligned, and results-oriented. This company operates nationally and is growing by the day.



SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

- Producing accurate, insightful reports that drive strategic decisions and improve campaign performance.

- Supporting the Marketing Operations, Sales Operations, and dashboard builders by ensuring seamless data integration, reporting accuracy, and actionable analysis across all platforms.

- Creating and maintaining dashboards that drive data transparency and actionable insights.





OBJECTIVES

- Dashboard Development: Build, manage, and optimize interactive dashboards in both Lookr studio and Power BI to support the marketing team.

- Ensure data accuracy, visual clarity, and usability of all reporting structures.

- Create and maintain reliable data sources, pipelines, and scheduled automations to streamline data collection and reporting processes.

- Collaborate cross functionally with other ops teams to ensure data integrity and scalability.

- Continuously improve reporting efficiency and adapt structures to evolving business needs.

- Present findings and recommendations cleary to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

- Translate complex datasets into digestible insights, visualizations, and narratives that drive clary and action.



COMPETENCIES

- Strong analytical skills, with the ability to extract, analyze, and report data from various sources.

- Proficiency in CRM tools, Zapier integrations, and marketing funnel workflows.

- Expertise in data reporting, KPI tracking, and leveraging analytics to drive marketing growth.

- Skilled in integrating and optimizing marketing platforms such as HubSpot, ClickFunnels, Kajabi, and Shopify.

- Experience with project management tools like Asana to track and organize campaigns.

- Strong problem-solving skills to address challenges in marketing operations and improve efficiency.

- Ability to write clear, compelling reports and presentations for stakeholders at all levels.

- A growth mindset with a willingness to learn and apply new technologies.



EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE

- 3-5 years in a reporting and analytics role.

- Hands on experience creating dashboards in both Lookr studio as well as Power Bi.



PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS

Prolonged periods of time sitting at a desk and computer



COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY



As an equal opportunity employer committed to meeting the needs of a multigenerational and multicultural workforce Cardone Ventures recognizes that a diverse staff, reflective of our community, is an integral and welcome part of a successful and ethical business. We hire local talent at all levels regardless of race, color, religion, age, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation or disability, and actively foster inclusion in all forms both within our company and across interactions with clients, candidates and partners.



If this position caught your eye, send us your resume! For best consideration, please complete the online application form:


/

title: Property Coordinator (Direct Hire)
description:
*KINDLY READ THE JOB DESCRIPTION & THE APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY.*



The Nabo Group is hiring Property Coordinator for our growing team, who enjoys solving problems, responsible, organized, detail oriented, and reliable to join our fun/hard working team. You must have excellent communication skills.



Key Responsibilities:

Customer Service and Issue Resolution:

- Respond to routine customer service requests promptly and appropriately.

- Address issues such as missed trash pickups or utility concerns, providing updates and progress reports to the Manager.

- Troubleshoot common problems and help facilitate resolutions.



Interdepartmental and Vendor Communication:

- Collaborate with internal departments, including accounting and maintenance, to ensure smooth coordination of services and accurate information sharing.

- Communicate effectively with external vendors regarding routine tasks, project updates, and general coordination.

- Serve as a point of contact between stakeholders to help streamline operations and resolve issues efficiently.



Portfolio Reporting:

- Prepare and send weekly and monthly reports on the status of the property portfolio to the Manager.

- Track and manage access information, including physical keys and access codes, and coordinate with internal teams, external vendors, and homeowners to ensure proper access to units as needed.



Communication and Notifications:

- Provide the Manager with the necessary information to send notices to homeowners regarding updates or community issues.

- Serve as a backup for phone calls, handling inquiries and directing them to the appropriate person as needed.



Financial Support and Oversight:

- Assist with accounts payable by reviewing and submitting invoices for approval.

- Maintain general understanding of general ledger management, ensuring expenses are properly tracked and categorized.

- Support budgeting processes by gathering relevant financial information and monitoring expenses.

- Communicate with the Manager regarding any financial discrepancies or questions related to invoices or expenses.



On-Call Participation:

- Participate in the on-call rotation one week per month to handle any urgent issues that arise outside of regular business hours.



Qualifications:

- Time Management: Ability to prioritize tasks, meet deadlines, and manage multiple projects simultaneously with minimal supervision.

- Organizational Skills: Strong organizational skills, with an emphasis on keeping detailed records and maintaining accurate documentation.

- Communication: Excellent verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to interact professionally with homeowners, tea
mbers, and vendors.

- Problem-Solving: Proactive in identifying issues and finding solutions to ensure that service requests and community concerns are addressed efficiently.

- Financial Acumen: General knowledge of accounts payable, invoice approval processes, general ledger tracking, and basic budgeting principles.

- Collaboration: Ability to work effectively across departments and with outside vendors to support HOA operations.

- Working Hours: Must be amenable to work in any US time zones



Benefits: HMO and PTOs after 90 days.

title: 4K Video Editor Needed ASAP - "TRSH Talk" Style (Reels & Full Episodes)
description:
Project Overview:



I am looking for a creative, fast-paced video editor to help launch a "Coming Soon" trailer and two episodes for my new series, Spill It. The editing style must strictly follow the vibe of the viral YouTube series TRSH Talk.



The Deliverables:



1x Mashup Trailer (9:16 Vertical): A "Coming Soon" teaser featuring both artists. High intense energy



2x Full Episodes: Individual edits for each artist.



3x Social Media Reels: High-energy highlights from the footage.



Technical Requirements



4K Resolution: All final exports must be in high-quality 4K resolution.



Audio Syncing: You MUST be able to perfectly sync the high-quality mic audio with the camera audio we recorded.



Multi-Cam/Cutting: Expertise in Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or DaVinci Resolve.



Fast Turnaround: Ability to work and communicate today.



Editing Style: The "TRSH Talk" Vibe



We are looking for high-retention, "viral" style editing. Please include the following "Easter Eggs" and effects:



The "Awkward Silence" Zoom: When an artist says something wild, do a slow, dramatic zoom on their face to make it funnier.



Shake Effects: Use a slight camera shake during loud, high-energy, or "hype" 
nts.



Bouncy Subtitles: Use bold, colorful, and animated captions (e.g., "The Bold Font") for key phrases and punchlines.



Dynamic Zooms: Frequent punch-ins and outs to emphasize reactions.



Sound Design: Pop-up sound effects (SFX), "whooshes," and comedic accents.



EXAMPLES: 








==


==



We are mainly posting this on IG and TIKTOk so need it to be engaging and eye catching. 



Will send you the logo for the show as well. 



How to Apply



Send a link to your portfolio or a specific clip you’ve edited in the "Retention" or "TRSH Talk" style.



Confirm you have the internet speed to handle 4K footage uploads/downloads.



Confirm you can start immediately.

title: Legal Support Specialist (Billing & Collections)
description:
About the Role



We are seeking a highly organized and proactive Legal Support Specialist to support a busy U.S. law practice.

This role will function as the attorney’s right-hand assistant, helping manage daily operations, client communications, billing follow-ups, and administrative legal support. The ideal candidate is professional, detail-oriented, and confident communicating with U.S. clients.

The position requires strong English communication skills and the ability to interact comfortably with clients via phone,
, and text.



Key Responsibilities



Legal Administrative Support

• Manage attorney calendar, reminders, and scheduling

• Assist with legal document organization and case file management

• Upload and organize documents within the practice management system

• Support preparation of legal documents, filings, and case materials

• Maintain organized case records and documentation

Client Intake & Communication

• Conduct initial intake calls with prospective clients

• Gather key details from clients and summarize information for the attorney

• Maintain professional communication with clients through phone,
, and text

• Provide summaries of client interviews to help the attorney prepare for consultations

Billing & Collections Support

• Assist with invoicing and payment follow-ups

• Contact clients regarding outstanding balances through calls, texts, and
s

• Help track payments received through various payment methods (Zelle, Cash App, etc.)

• Ensure client payment status is updated and documented

Operations & Practice Support

• Help keep the attorney organized and on schedule

• Track outstanding tasks and reminders

• Assist with general office administration and workflow management



Required Qualifications



• 2–4 years of experience as a Legal Assistant, Executive Assistant, or Legal Administrative Assistant

• Experience supporting billing, invoicing, or collections follow-up

• Strong English communication skills with a near-native accent

• Experience communicating with U.S. clients via phone and

• Prior experience using Clio

• Strong organizational and time-management skills

• Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks

• Professional phone presence and client communication skills



Preferred Qualifications



• Experience working in a U.S. law firm

• Familiarity with family law, bankruptcy, or criminal law environments

• Experience handling client intake calls



To be considered for the role:



-Please send the link of your resume

-Your expected hourly rate

-30 second voice introduction

title: Overseas Remote Construction Estimator
description:
Construction Estimator will be responsible for preparing accurate and competitive cost estimates for a variety of projects. This role requires close coordination with project managers, architects, engineers, and subcontractors to ensure timely and precise bid submissions and budgeting. Must have experience with New Jersey/New York (or Tri-State Area) local pricing. 



Key Responsibilities:

• Research and prepare detailed, competitive cost estimates for construction projects.

• Define and clarify the scope of work based on project plans, specifications, and client requirements.

• Perform cost build-up, including pricing for labor, materials, equipment, and subcontractor work.

• Conduct thorough risk analysis and incorporate potential risk costs into estimates.

• Prepare and submit accurate bid proposals within deadlines.

• Identify labor, material, and time requirements for each project.

• Analyze and evaluate subcontractor and supplier offers to ensure cost-efficiency and compliance.

• Review and assess competing bids for scope accuracy, pricing, and qualifications.

• Ensure high levels of accuracy in cost estimating and pricing.

• Input and maintain cost data in the company’s estimating database for future reference and benchmarking.

• Maintain strict client confidentiality and handle sensitive project data with professionalism.

• Build and maintain strong working relationships with subcontractors, vendors, and suppliers.

• Prepare and manage the estimating schedule to align with project timelines and bid due dates.

• Review and interpret construction drawings, blueprints, and technical specifications.

• Select appropriate estimating metrics and benchmarks to ensure accurate and consistent estimates.

Qualifications:

• 3+ years of experience in construction estimating, preferably in both residential and commercial sectors.

• Strong understanding of construction materials, methods, and market pricing.

• Exceptional attention to detail and organizational skills.

• Self-motivated with the ability to work independently in a remote environment.

• Degree in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, or a related field is a plus.

• Familiarity with New Jersey/New York building codes and local pricing trends is a must.

title: Business Analyst
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE:



The Business Analyst role requires dedication, detailed work, and integrity. The right person would need to be adept in the business analysis tools and have solid experience in business processes in these key industries: finance, manufacturing, and IT.  





RESPONSIBILITIES:

In this role you'll get to...



-establish and document business processes

-perform requirement elicitation through different channels of discovery

-assist in requirement planning and management

-perform requirement analysis and documentation

-identify business process gaps and align solutions to project goals

-support the project team using business analysis tools

-establish communication services across stakeholders

-detail Scrum stories in standard format

-participate in daily stand-ups and capture information

-assist in grooming/sizing of stories

-facilitate testing/product performance recording

-detail product reviews and releases

-complete project deliverables with quality and in a timely manner

-maintain the storage and retrieval of all project communications data and business metrics, as applicable

-gather information to establish the current business processes of  the client to establish current state and determine painpoints and improvements. 

-perform other adhoc tasks related to the role





REQUIREMENTS:

On day one we'll expect you to...



-have 5+ years experience in Business Analysis field

-have 3+ years experience in the Information Technology, Manufacturing, or Finance industry

-have a bachelor's degree in Business Management, Information Technology, Management Information Systems or relevant field required

-have the ability to analyze, map, and document business processes

-be knowledgeable in business and management principles involved in strategic planning, resource allocation, and production methods

-have a proven experience in business analysis tools

-have the ability to facilitate testing/product performance recording

-have excellent verbal and written communication skills

-have mastery of presentation applications and spreadsheets

-ERP systems experience (NetSuite, SAP, SAP Business One, Acumatica, MS Dynamics 365, etc..)

-exposure to Agile projects







NOTE: This is a remote (work from home) job. Open for part-time position.

title: Estimation and Contractor Recruitment Specialist
description:
Role Overview:



The Estimation and Contractor Recruitment Specialist is responsible for creating comprehensive, detailed scopes of work based on project requirements, identifying qualified contractors capable of performing each specific role, and managing the quotation process to ensure accurate, competitive pricing. The ideal candidate will leverage exceptional organizational skills, numerical proficiency, and outstanding communication abilities to ensure seamless coordination and clarity across all project phases.



Key Responsibilities:



- Thoroughly review and analyze project details to clearly define and document comprehensive scopes of work.

- Develop precise scopes of work tailored to each contractor role, clearly specifying all tasks, materials, skills, and expectations.

- Identify, recruit, and engage qualified contractors who possess the necessary skill sets and experience to successfully fulfill defined scopes of work.

- Solicit and obtain detailed quotations from multiple contractors (ideally three per role) for each defined scope.

- Critically evaluate contractor quotations for accuracy, completeness, competitiveness, and alignment with project objectives.

- Compile and present organized summaries of quotations, contractor capabilities, and recommendations for selection to project stakeholders.

- Maintain meticulous records of scopes, quotations, contractor communications, and evaluation criteria.

- Qualifications and Essential Skills:

- Demonstrated organizational skills with an ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines effectively.

- Strong numerical aptitude and analytical skills to interpret and compare contractor quotes accurately.

- Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical requirements clearly and precisely.

- Proven ability to independently problem-solve, demonstrating common sense and critical thinking.

- Self-motivated, proactive, and capable of navigating complex challenges to deliver effective solutions.



** Must be comfortable engaging in detailed discussions and negotiations with contractors to clearly define project requirements, ensure complete mutual understanding of the scope, and achieve optimal pricing. **





Personal Attributes:



- Highly organized and detail-oriented.

- Excellent interpersonal skills and emotional intelligence.

- Dependable and results-driven, capable of working autonomously.

- Strong sense of ownership and accountability in task execution.



This role is critical to ensuring precise contractor selection and project execution, significantly impacting overall project success through clear communication, meticulous preparation, and thorough evaluation.



Working hours are from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EST; please ensure you are comfortable and available to work within this schedule.

title: Ecommerce Graphic Designer – Toy Lifestyle Compositing Specialist (Photoshop Expert)
description:
About the Role:

We’re looking for a highly skilled Ecommerce Graphic Designer who can turn simple product renders into eye-catching, realistic lifestyle images for toy products. Your superpower is Photoshop, not pushing an AI button and hoping for magic. You understand compositing, lighting, shadows, perspective, textures, and realism so the final image feels authentic, playful, and emotionally engaging.



You’ll be creating visuals for:

- Amazon product listings

- Ecommerce galleries

- Toy packaging

- Instruction manuals



Your work should make kids excited and parents confident. Think fun, safe, imaginative, and premium.



Key Responsibilities:

- Create high-quality lifestyle images from product renders or basic photos using advanced Photoshop compositing techniques.

- Place products naturally into scenes with children and play environments while maintaining realistic lighting, scale, and perspective.

- Design packaging layouts and instruction manuals that are clear, visually appealing, and easy to understand.

- Produce Amazon-ready images that follow marketplace standards and maximize conversion.

- Retouch and enhance images while maintaining product accuracy and brand consistency.

- Collaborate with marketing and product teams.

- Use AI tools when helpful but rely primarily on Photoshop.

- Optimize files for print and digital use.



Required Skills & Experience:

- Advanced Adobe Photoshop skills.

- Proven experience creating lifestyle images from product renders.

- Ecommerce experience, especially Amazon.

- Packaging and instruction manual design experience.

- Strong understanding of what visually attracts kids and parents.

- High attention to detail.

- Strong file organization and workflow discipline.



Bonus Skills:

- Experience in toy or consumer product brands.

- AI tools knowledge.

- Basic illustration or 3D knowledge.

- Print production experience.



How to Apply:

Submit resume and portfolio with before-and-after Photoshop samples.

title: NZ Accounting Specialist (15-20 hours per week)
description:
Start Out Savvy is a boutique New Zealand accounting practice specialising in service businesses.

We help business owners get on top of their numbers, their cashflow, and their growth. 

We're bold, practical, and genuinely good to work for - and we're looking for an experienced NZ Accounting Specialist to join our small, high-performing offshore team.



?? Please read before applying

This role requires direct, hands-on experience preparing New Zealand GST returns and annual accounts. 

If your NZ knowledge is theoretical only, please do not apply. All shortlisted applicants will complete a paid test task before any offer is made.



The Role (15 hrs per week, Monday–Thursday only):

You'll be working alongside our NZ-based team preparing client files to review-ready standard, including:



Preparing NZ annual financial statements and tax returns in Xero and Xero Workpapers

Preparing GST returns for trades businesses (monthly and two-monthly filers)

Xero reconciliation, coding and bookkeeping for small business clients

Provisional tax calculations and IRD filing preparation

Payroll support for clients with employees

Weekly status update via Google Chat every Thursday — proactive communication is non-negotiable



Who You Are:



CPA or equivalent qualification preferred

Minimum 3 years hands-on experience preparing NZ GST returns and annual accounts independently

Worked for a NZ accounting practice or outsource provider (not study only — real client files)

Daily Xero user - must be Xero Advisor Certified 

Strong working knowledge of NZ IRD, tax codes, and filing requirements

Excellent written English — clear team communication is essential

Able to work independently without hand-holding — you know what to do when you open a client file

Available Monday–Thursday, 15 hours per week from early April 2026

If you are currently working full-time for another employer, do not apply



Bonus Points If You Have:



Experience with trades businesses, contractors or construction clients

Cashflow & management reporting

Familiarity with NZ company structures, shareholder salaries and dividend treatment



Why Join Us:



Guaranteed 15 hours per week from day one — stable, reliable income

Weekly pay via Wise in NZD

5 paid cultural leave days per year — your holidays, your choice

2 paid team recharge days per year — we rest together as a team

Christmas payment paid first week of December in lieu of 13th month payment

4-day work week — we finish Thursday, every week, always

Long-term role with written progression milestones — real growth, not vague promises

A small, genuine team that values your contribution and tells you so



How To Apply:

Send your application with:



A cover message (max 200 words) covering: how long you have been preparing NZ GST returns and annual accounts, which NZ firms you have worked for, and your current weekly availability

Your CV with a clear work history listing every role, dates, and specifically what NZ accounting work you performed

Your answer to this question — "A trades client has 180 unreconciled transactions in Xero with a GST return due. 

What do you do first, and what are the three most common coding errors you look for in a trades business?"



Shortlisted applicants will be invited to complete a paid test task (USD $15, 48-hour turnaround). 

This is how we assess real skill — not just what you tell us.

We review applications as they arrive. When we find the right person, the role is filled. Apply promptly.

description:
Registered Nurse / Clinical Quality Assurance Registered Nurse



Job Type: Full-Time

Work Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM Pacific Time, with a 1-hour break

Salary: $1,300 per month



Job Overview: 

We are seeking a Registered Nurse / Clinical Quality Assurance Registered Nurse with experience in Medicare home health documentation and PDGM coding. Experience with Kinnser/WellSky Home Health or similar home health EMR systems is preferred. In this role, you will review OASIS assessments, clinical visit notes, and patient charts to ensure accuracy, compliance, and readiness for submission. Responsibilities include conducting QA reviews, validating OASIS data, reviewing diagnoses and PDGM coding, ensuring Plans of Care and physician orders align with clinical documentation, and maintaining survey-ready records. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, organized, and able to collaborate closely with clinical staff to ensure timely, compliant, and high-quality documentation.



Key Responsibilities:

-Quality Assurance & Clinical Review

-Conduct QA for all OASIS assessments and clinical visit notes

-Perform OASIS scrubbing and validation prior to submission to iQIES

-Ensure OASIS accuracy, consistency, and compliance with CMS requirements

-Review documentation for skilled need, homebound status, goal progression, and appropriate interventions

-Coding & PDGM Review

-Code charts accurately according to the patient's diagnoses

-Ensure appropriate primary diagnosis selection for PDGM grouping

-Review comorbidities and secondary diagnoses

-Identify potential LUPA risks and documentation issues

-Plan of Care & Orders Review

-Review and finalize the Plan of Care (485)

-Ensure physician orders align with the clinical documentation and plan of care

-Track and review physician orders and unsigned orders

-Documentation & Compliance

-Complete 30-day summaries

-Ensure charts meet Medicare Conditions of Participation

-Maintain survey-ready documentation

-Identify and flag compliance risks

-Operational Oversight

-Monitor documentation timeliness for SOC, recertifications, and visit notes

-Provide feedback to clinicians when corrections are required

-Maintain consistent communication with the Director of Nursing (DON) and clinical staff



Skills & Qualifications:

Registered Nurse (RN) license required.

Experience working in home health quality assurance, clinical documentation review, or chart auditing.

Strong knowledge of OASIS documentation standards and CMS Medicare home health requirements.

Experience with Kinnser/WellSky Home Health or similar home health EMR systems (preferred).

Solid understanding of PDGM coding and diagnosis review for home health services.

Ability to identify documentation gaps, compliance risks, and coding inaccuracies.

Strong attention to detail, analytical skills, and organizational abilities.

Excellent written communication skills for providing feedback to clinicians and clinical staff.

Ability to work independently in a remote environment and complete QA reviews and coding tasks within required timelines.

Ability to collaborate effectively with nursing leadership and clinical teams.

title: Rockstar Accountants
description:
FULL-TIME NIGHT SHIFT | Remote | For High-Performance Professionals Only



Pivotl is seeking an exceptional Remote Accountant to join our specialized team providing advanced accounting services across the United States. This is a FULL-TIME, NIGHT SHIFT position designed for seasoned professionals who thrive under pressure and deliver excellence consistently.



IMPORTANT: This is NOT a side job or flexible arrangement. We need dedicated professionals accustomed to night shift operations who can commit to full-time hours. If you're seeking part-time work or are uncomfortable with night schedules, please do not apply.



What Sets This Role Apart

- 4-day work week, 3 days off - Maximize your work-life balance

- Premium compensation for proven high performers

- Comprehensive benefits: Paid vacation/sick leave, extensive paid training,

- Complete autonomy in a remote environment - perfect for self-directed professionals

- Specialized focus on HOME SERVICE industries (HVAC, Solar, and related sectors)



The Challenge - Are You Ready?



We need a financial data cleanup specialist who can handle:

- Granular-level financial data reconciliation across complex business structures (Profit & Loss, Balance Sheet, AR, Vendor Recon)

- High-pressure deadline management while maintaining 100?curacy

- Independent problem-solving of complex accounting discrepancies

- Proactive identification and resolution of financial irregularities

- Core Responsibilities for Elite Performers

- Execute comprehensive book-cleanup projects with tight deadlines

- Perform detailed adjusting entries with a deep understanding of tax implications

- Master QuickBooks Online for complex multi-entity structures

- Conduct advanced data analysis using Excel and Google Sheets

- Manage full-cycle AR/AP, invoicing, payroll, and bank reconciliations

- Deliver precise categorization and accounting across diverse business models



Non-Negotiable Requirements

- Proven track record in US accounting with demonstrable results

- Expert-level proficiency in QuickBooks Online

- Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills for complex data manipulation

- Exceptional English communication - written and verbal

- Night shift availability - this is not flexible

- Autonomous work style - you must be self-directed and proactive

- Competitive Advantages (Preferred)

- Industry background in HVAC, solar, generators, roofing, foundations, or electrical

- Construction/contracting industry experience highly valued

- Experience using any of the following: Service Titan, Housecall Pro, Sera, Jobber, Service Trade, Acculynx, etc

- Experience using Keeper.app is a plus



Who Should Apply

- Rock stars only. We want professionals who:

- Thrive in high-pressure, deadline-driven environments

- Work autonomously without constant supervision

- Have extensive experience cleaning up complex historical financial data

- Take ownership of results and deliver consistent excellence

- Are committed to full-time night shift operations



Who Should NOT Apply

- Those seeking part-time or flexible arrangements

- Professionals are uncomfortable with night shift schedules

- Anyone looking for a "side job" or supplemental income

- Those requiring extensive hand-holding or micromanagement

- Ready to prove you're among the elite?



Apply Here:

title: Video Editor and Social Media Manager
description:
Job Overview:

I am the CEO of Pauv (
), an Austin-based startup building the "stock market for people." I am looking for a high-energy, creative partner to help me scale our social presence and reach out to potential partners (musicians, athletes, and streamers).



Responsibilities:



- Video Editing (CapCut): I will provide ~3 minutes of raw footage daily. You will edit this into a high-retention, <60 second short-form video (TikTok/Reels style) with captions, cuts, and engaging pacing. Expected output: 2-3 videos per day. I will provide you with video editing software.

- Social Outreach: Engage with target accounts on X, TikTok, and Instagram. This includes leaving thoughtful comments and sending personalized DMs to potential "Issuers" based on scripts/guidelines I provide.

- Daily Sync: Report on engagement metrics and lead status.



Requirements:



- Expertise in CapCut: You must be able to edit quickly and follow "viral" styles (hooks, captions, jump cuts).

- Excellent English: Your written English must be flawless and conversational. No "bot-like" messaging.

- Reliability: You must be able to deliver edits within 24 hours of receiving raw footage.

- Interest in Finance/Creators: You should understand the concept of "investing in people" or the creator economy.



We will ONLY look at applications who have a portfolio linked with short form video content. 



There will also be a short video editing test and a conversationalist test before moving on.

Our agency provides full-service digital marketing including SEO, PPC, Google Ads, social media, and web design. title: Senior WordPress Developer & Site Manager (Advanced Coding + Performance Focus)
description:
## **Job Overview**



We are hiring a **Senior WordPress Developer** to fully manage and protect a **mission-critical WordPress + WooCommerce website**. This is a **full-time, long-term role** for someone with **deep technical expertise**, not a junior or entry-level WordPress assistant.



This site is content-heavy, SEO-driven, and compliance-sensitive. We need someone who can **build, optimize, troubleshoot, and maintain the site at an expert level without breaking things**.



**Only apply if you have strong coding experience and advanced WordPress knowledge.**



---



## **Core Responsibilities**



### **Advanced WordPress Development**



* Architect and maintain a complex WordPress site using **Kadence Theme** and **Gutenberg block editor**

* Develop and customize blocks, templates, and layouts (no page-builder-only users)

* Work directly with **PHP, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS** (not just basic edits)

* Create and manage **custom post types, taxonomies, and templates**

* Safely implement structural changes without site downtime



### **Advanced Troubleshooting & Stability**



* Diagnose and resolve complex WordPress, plugin, and WooCommerce issues

* Debug PHP errors, database issues, and server-level conflicts

* Handle plugin conflicts and compatibility issues proactively

* Maintain staging ? production workflows correctly



### **Performance & Page Speed Optimization**



* Optimize for **Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, INP)**

* Advanced experience with:



  * Caching (server-level + plugin-based)

  * Image optimization

  * Script and asset optimization

  * Database optimization

* Experience with tools such as **WP Rocket, LiteSpeed, Cloudflare, Redis, or similar**

* Maintain fast load times on content-heavy pages



### **SEO & Technical Structure Support**



* Implement advanced SEO structures (clean URLs, internal linking, schema)

* Work with **Rank Math SEO** at a technical level

* Ensure proper indexing, canonicalization, and crawl efficiency

* Support large-scale content publishing without SEO issues



---



## **Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)**



* **3+ years of professional WordPress development experience**

* **Strong coding skills**:



  * PHP (WordPress core & theme customization)

  * JavaScript

  * Advanced HTML & CSS

* **Kadence Theme + Gutenberg blocks experience**

* Advanced WooCommerce experience

* Proven experience optimizing WordPress sites for **speed and performance**

* Experience managing **high-risk or high-traffic websites**



???? **This role is NOT for:**



* Beginners or junior WordPress users

* VA-style WordPress assistants

* Page-builder-only developers

* “I know basic HTML/CSS” applicants



---



## **Work Requirements**



* **Full-time (40 hours/week)**

* **Must work during US Central Time business hours**

* Reliable internet and professional communication skills

* Long-term commitment only



---



## **Nice-to-Have (Strong Plus)**



* Experience with regulated, research, or compliance-sensitive websites

* Experience with WooCommerce at scale

* Familiarity with hosting environments like WP Engine

* Experience with Git or version control

* Experience optimizing large content libraries



---



## **Compensation**



* Competitive pay based on skill level and experience

* Long-term stability and consistent work

* Opportunity to become the **primary technical owner** of a growing platform



---



## **How to Apply**



Please include:



1. **Detailed WordPress development experience**

2. Examples of **advanced WordPress sites** you’ve worked on

3. Your **experience with Kadence and Gutenberg**

4. Your **page speed optimization experience** (tools + results)

5. Your **availability during US Central Time**

6. Your **expected monthly salary**



Applications without proof of advanced experience will not be considered.



---



### **Final Note**



This site cannot break. We are looking for someone who understands that responsibility and has the experience to handle it.



---



If you want next:



* A **technical screening test**

* A **trial task that exposes weak candidates**

* A **salary range recommendation for Philippines senior devs**

* A **first-week onboarding checklist**



Just say the word.

title: US Recruiting Sourcer / Talent Researcher – Remote | $400–$600 USD
description:
US Talent Researcher / Recruiting Sourcer – Remote | $400–$600 USD

Discover the professionals leading major construction projects across the United States.

Help identify the Project Managers, Superintendents, and leaders who build hospitals, stadiums, and large commercial developments.



If you enjoy research, solving puzzles, and finding hard-to-locate professionals online, this role will be exciting and rewarding.

You’ll work with a U.S. executive recruiting team that places leadership talent at construction companies across the country.

This role is perfect for someone who enjoys digging deep, uncovering talent, and building organized pipelines of candidates.



Strong performers can grow into Senior Sourcer or full-cycle Recruiter roles over time.



***What You'll Be Doing***

• Research and identify construction professionals across the United States

• Use LinkedIn, ZoomInfo/Apollo, and advanced search techniques to find candidates

• Build structured candidate pipelines for active recruiting projects

• Organize and track talent information inside Google Sheets or CRM

• Conduct basic qualification research (title, location, experience)

• Work closely with recruiters to support open roles and hiring targets



This role is research-driven and detail-focused.



***What Success Looks Like***

Within your first 90 days you will learn how to:

• Day 30: Identify 30–50 qualified professionals per week

• Day 60: Support 8–12 candidate submissions per week

• Day 90: Build consistent talent pipelines for multiple recruiting projects



***Ideal Background***

• Strong internet research skills; organized and detail-oriented

• Curious and persistent when searching for information

• Comfortable working with spreadsheets and online tools

• Strong written English communication



***Helpful but not required***

• Experience sourcing U.S. candidates

• Recruiting or talent research experience

• Familiarity with LinkedIn search or Boolean searches



***Compensation and Growth***

Base salary: $400–$600 USD per month depending on experience

Career path toward Senior Sourcer or full-cycle Recruiter roles



***Work Schedule***

• US Eastern Time schedule (Philippines night shift) • Monday to Friday

• Minimum 50 Mbps internet connection with backup

• Quiet workspace and reliable headset



***How to Apply (Follow Exactly)***

Subject line: SOURCER – [Your Name]

• Include your resume and  a brief recording introducing yourself and highlighting your sourcing experience

• Submit one Boolean string for a Senior Project Manager (construction) and explain it briefly

• Attach a three-company target list (city, role focus, reason for fit)

• Provide a five-line outreach message you would send to a candidate



If you’re ready to build a meaningful career in recruitment, sharpen your research skills, and help shape the teams behind America’s most ambitious construction projects — we want to hear from you.

title: Sr. Web Developer-PH
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE: 



THIS IS A PART-TIME TO FULLTIME ROLE (will start as part-time for 60-90 days then transition to fulltime)!



We’re looking for a Senior Web Developer to join our dev team. You thrive building beautiful, high-performing websites and digital experiences for top-tier brands. You can lead multiple projects, mentor other developers, and communicate clearly with clients while still getting deep in the code when needed.



RESPONSIBILITIES: 



In this role, you'll get to...



lead development of WordPress using ACF, custom blocks, Twig templating, custom post types, and block-based themes, with performance, accessibility, SEO, and high-quality execution built in

build projects with headless CMS platforms like Contentful, Storyblok, or WordPress as a headless backend, leveraging REST API or GraphQL, and using frontend frameworks like Next.js or Nuxt.js with headless architectures

create pixel-perfect, fully functioning frontends using Tailwind CSS, GSAP , and other modern frontend technologies, ensuring responsive, interactive, and high-performance experiences

integrate backend systems, APIs, CRMs, marketing automation (Salesforce, Marketo, HubSpot, careers platforms), AI tools, and eCommerce solutions

own CI/CD workflows and deploy sites across Vercel, Netlify, AWS, Kinsta, and WP Engine, including edge/CDN optimizations

serve as a primary technical point of contact for internal teams (PM, design, SEO) and clients, translating complex ideas into clear recommendations

mentor junior developers and provide technical leadership across multiple projects

collaborate using Figma, Slack, Teamwork, and Pastel to bring designs to life efficiently

perform other ad hoc duties related to the role as assigned

REQUIREMENTS: 



On day one, we'll expect you to...



have 7+ years of web development experience, ideally in agency environments

have strong full-stack expertise, comfortable with both frontend and backend

have deep knowledge of modern WordPress, headless CMS, Next.js or Nuxt.js, Tailwind, GSAP, CI/CD, deployments, and integrations

have proven experience leading multiple projects and teams, with excellent client-facing communication

have hands-on experience with API integrations, marketing automation, AI tools, eCommerce, SEO, security, and DevOps

be detail-oriented, proactive, and capable of translating technical decisions into actionable solutions

be open to part-time to fulltime opportunity

have a fully functional and up-to-date computer with which to perform duties

be willing to install next-generation endpoint protection on the computer

be willing to work in US Pacific time zone (8:00am-5:00pm PST) corresponding to 12:00am -9:00am PH time

be a current resident of the Philippines and legally able to perform work from there

What separates the best from the rest:



advanced GSAP/ animations experience

mentorship, client management, and cross-team collaboration experience

experience optimizing performance, accessibility, SEO, security, and DevOps at scale

Edge and CDN strategy experience for modern web projects

NOTES:



Benefits await full-time contractors. 

This is a remote (work from home) position.



TO APPLY: ** DO NOT SEND YOUR APPLICATION HERE IN INDEED** Follow the instructions below:



1. COPY & PASTE this link to your browser: 

2. Fill out the form and submit your resume to our system.



We will reach out to you via
 using our system!

Thank you!

title: Tech VA - LinkedIn B2B Outreach Strategist & Campaign Architect
description:
We’re looking for an experienced LinkedIn B2B Outreach Strategist to support client campaigns across healthcare, life sciences, and complex B2B industries.



This is a fully remote, independent contractor role that may be part-time, full-time, or project-based, depending on fit and availability.



You’ll design and launch strategy-first LinkedIn outreach campaigns that engage senior decision-makers and generate qualified conversations, not spam.



If you know how to turn LinkedIn into a serious B2B growth channel, this role is for you.



What You’ll Do



Design LinkedIn outreach strategies based on ICPs and buyer personas



Set up and manage campaigns using LinkedIn automation tools + Sales Navigator



Build personalized, multi-step outreach sequences for consultative B2B sales

title: ADMIN & OPERATIONS COORDINATOR
description:
About the Role

We are a growing real estate and property management company looking for a detail-oriented, proactive Admin & Operations Coordinator to join our remote team. This role is the operational backbone of our business — the connective tissue between our Property Management, Maintenance, Finance, and Sales teams.

You will own day-to-day financial tracking, vendor payments, KPI dashboards, client communications, and documentation — freeing our property managers and sales agents to focus on what they do best.



Key Responsibilities



????  Financial Tracking & Admin

• Track rent payments daily; log receipts, flag delinquencies, and send payment reminders to tenants

• Receive, log, and route vendor invoices for approval

• Process approved payments — check creation and/or ACH initiation

• Perform monthly financial reconciliation — match payments to ledgers

• Support preparation of monthly owner statements

• Assist with year-end reporting including 1099 preparation and expense summaries





????  Dashboards & KPI Tracking

• Maintain live operational dashboards (occupancy rate, delinquency rate, maintenance response time, vacancy days)

• Deliver weekly and monthly KPI reports to leadership

• Track portfolio-level performance metrics across all managed properties

• Proactively flag anomalies, trends, and areas of concern





????  Phone & Communication Handling

• Answer inbound calls and route to the appropriate tea
mber (property manager, maintenance coordinator, or sales agent)

• Take detailed messages and log call notes in the company CRM or property management software

• Follow up on open items and outstanding requests from calls

• Manage shared inboxes and ensure timely responses





????  Sales & Agent Support

• Track deal pipeline and transaction status for sales agents

• Coordinate and manage transaction documentation — purchase agreements, disclosures, and addendums

• Manage e-signature workflows via DocuSign or DotLoop

• Track commission milestones and support payment processing

• Monitor contingency and closing deadlines; alert agents proactively





????  Lead Management & Showing Coordination

• Respond promptly to inbound leads from Zillow, 
, company website, and other sources

• Log and track all leads in the CRM; assign to the appropriate agent or property manager

• Follow up with prospects via phone,
, or text to qualify interest and gather requirements

• Schedule property showings and rental tours — coordinate availability between prospects and agents

• Send showing confirmations, reminders, and follow-up messages to prospects

• Manage and update the showing calendar to avoid conflicts and double-bookings

• Track lead status through the pipeline — new, contacted, showing scheduled, application received, closed

• Nurture cold or unresponsive leads with drip follow-up sequences

• Compile weekly lead activity and conversion reports for leadership





????  Documentation & Records Management

• Create and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) as processes are defined

• Organize digital files by property, tenant, vendor, and transaction

• Draft notices, letters, and communication templates as needed

• Ensure lease files, vendor contracts, and agent agreements are current and organized

• Support onboarding of new properties and vendors into company systems





Requirements

Must-Have

• 2+ years of experience in an administrative, operations coordinator, or virtual assistant role

• Strong experience with financial tracking, invoice management, or bookkeeping support

• Excellent written and verbal English communication skills

• Highly organized with strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities

• Proficient with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive) or Microsoft Office

• Comfortable working remotely and independently with minimal supervision

• Reliable internet connection and a dedicated, professional home office setup



Nice-to-Have

• Experience with property management software (AppFolio, Buildium, or Rent Manager)

• Familiarity with real estate transactions, lease documentation, or MLS

• Experience with QuickBooks or similar accounting tools

• Experience with DocuSign, DotLoop, or e-signature platforms

• Background in real estate, property management, or financial services

title: Client Experience & Operations Assistant
description:
I run a specialist shoulder rehabilitation consultancy helping professionals overcome long-standing shoulder pain through a structured 12-week program. As the business grows, I’m looking for a highly organised and professional Client Experience and Operations Assistant to help manage enquiries, client communication, and operational tasks.



This role is important because you will often be the first point of contact for potential clients, so communication must be clear, calm, and professional. This is a remote role and intended to be a long-term position for the right person as the business continues to grow.



Your main responsibility will be helping manage enquiries and keeping the operational side of the business organised. Typical tasks will include:



• Responding to incoming enquiries from social media

• Asking a few initial qualifying questions

• Sending intake forms to potential clients

• Organising leads inside our CRM system

• Requesting additional information such as posture photos or MRI reports

• Preparing simple lead summaries before strategy calls

• Keeping the client pipeline organised

• Updating our CRM as leads move through the process

• Providing a simple weekly report of enquiries and leads



The ideal candidate is organised, reliable, and comfortable communicating with professionals and executives. You should be confident working independently and able to follow structured processes carefully.



Requirements include:



• Excellent written English

• Clear spoken English

• Strong attention to detail

• Previous experience in virtual assistance, client management, or operations support

• Reliable internet connection and a quiet workspace



Communication style is very important in this role. Our clients are professionals, so messages should feel:



• calm

• clear

• professional

• respectful



They should never feel overly casual or rushed.



Initially the role will involve 10–20 hours per week, with the potential for this to increase as the business grows. Compensation will be $700–$1,000 per month depending on experience. This is intended to be a long-term position for the right person.



To apply, please complete the following steps:



Record a short 2-minute Loom video introducing yourself and explaining your previous experience.



Answer the following scenario in writing:



A potential client sends the message:

“Hi, I’ve had shoulder pain for years and nothing has worked. Can you help?”



Please write the response you would send.



Begin your application with the word RESET so I know you have read the full job description carefully.



This is not a generic administrative role. The person hired for this position will be helping manage the client experience for a specialist consultancy and ensuring that potential clients receive clear and professional communication from the 
nt they first enquire about the program.

title: Senior SEO and Marketing Specialist
description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Senior SEO Specialist to join our dynamic CNSS marketing team. The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of search engine optimization strategies and techniques, with a proven track record of driving organic traffic and improving search rankings. This role requires a blend of technical expertise and creative problem-solving skills to enhance our online presence and maximize the effectiveness of our digital marketing efforts.



TRACKING SOFTWARE IS MANDATORY FOR ALL OUR EMPLOYEES. WE USE TERAMIND



Duties

Minimum 3 years of experience

Develop and implement comprehensive SEO strategies to increase organic search visibility and traffic.

Conduct keyword research to identify high-value opportunities for content creation and optimization.

Analyze website performance using tools such as Google Analytics, Google Search Console, and Google Tag Manager to monitor traffic trends and user behavior.

Optimize website content, structure, and on-page elements (including HTML, CSS, and JavaScript) for improved search engine rankings.

Collaborate with the content team to create SEO-friendly content that aligns with business goals and audience needs.

Perform technical SEO audits to identify issues affecting site performance and provide actionable recommendations for improvement.

Stay updated on industry trends, algorithm changes, and best practices in SEO to ensure ongoing optimization efforts are effective.

Manage E-commerce SEO initiatives to enhance product visibility and drive sales through organic search channels.

Utilize Adobe tools for design enhancements that support SEO strategies.

Build high-quality backlinks and improve domain authority

Plan, launch, and manage Google Ads campaigns

Requirements



Proven experience as an SEO Specialist or similar role, with a strong portfolio demonstrating successful projects.

Proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and WordPress; familiarity with website development is a plus.

Experience with E-commerce platforms and optimizing product listings for search engines.

In-depth knowledge of Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Google Tag Manager, and other relevant SEO tools.

Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and make data-driven decisions.

Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams.

A proactive approach to problem-solving with attention to detail.

If you are passionate about SEO and eager to make a significant impact in a fast-paced environment, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.

title: Mortgage Loan Writer | Credit Analyst | Parabroker - Remote Working from home
description:
Key Details:



• Schedule: Monday to Friday, New Zealand hours

• Work Environment: Secure work from home

• No agency – work direct for the New Zealand company



The Company



Matthew Dawe Mortgages is a New Zealand based young and growing mortgage company. We are passionate about helping clients get finance for their dream home, first home or investment property. We take the stress and confusion out of the mortgage process by helping clients get approved, negotiate with lenders & provide financial advice along the way. After a growth period we need someone to help with analysing client documents, writing deals, proposals & preparing servicing calculators. This involves reviewing & understanding a clients supporting information and packaging this up for presentation to lenders to help the client get approved. There are other credit related tasks such as research & property/security tasks as well as pricing & structure tasks. We have a team of young, happy, high performers, who are committed to their work and who support each other to succeed. 



The Role



We are looking for a gun Loan Writer | Credit Analyst. You’ll analyse the client’s supporting documents, research lender criteria and package up/ produce a lender proposal/diary note and servicing calculator. Your goal will be to accurately & timely assist the Mortgage broker | Financial adviser to help clients get finance to purchase a home whilst providing excellent attention to detail along the way. 



You will have at least 18 months experience as a Loan writer | Credit analyst for a New Zealand or Australian based mortgage broker. The right person for this role will know how to package a deal, and can research lender criteria, understand the clients deposit, income, bank statements and other supporting documents, and produce a thorough proposal and servicing calculator. This involves carefully checking your work and following a process. 

Your success will enable us to help more clients, by freeing up/ supporting the Mortgage broker | Financial adviser. Ideally you will have 18 months+ experience as a loan writer analysing client documents, packaging & writing up deals. We are after a self-starter who is driven by constant improving & learning as a personal philosophy, has a high attention to detail and takes pride in their work. This role would suit someone who takes the initiative, is adaptable and can follow a system to a successful result. 



Requirements



• Experience: Proven 18 months+ experience working for a New Zealand or Australian based mortgage broker as a loan writer | credit analyst analysing a clients supporting documents, researching lender criteria and packaging up a proposal/diary note & servicing calculators is a prerequisite for this role. 

• Know mortgage loan products, bank platforms, and processes.

• Understand bank/lender policies and procedures.

• Skills: Excellent verbal and written English communication skills

• Attention to detail: Being able to focus

• Characteristics: You are highly ethical, take accountability & ownership of your work 

• Learning & improving as a self-philosophy, being able to ‘dig in’ when the learning/going gets tough to achieve improvement & a well earnt success.

• Ability to follow a process, be adaptable to learn our process, read checklists & watch videos carefully then implement this learning into completing tasks for clients with a high degree of accuracy. 

• Strong computer skills: Strong computer skills are essential 

• Highly organised & proficient at prioritising tasks

• Team player ability to work under pressure & prioritise appropriately. 

• You are smart, detail orientated, have strong sense of pride/checking work and are able to focus. 



Benefits



At Matthew Dawe Mortgages, we ask a lot, which is why we give so much in return. We offer:



• Great pay: 72,000 PHP/month, 40 hours per week, 5am – 2pm PHST (with an hour of breaks, 8 hours worked/day) Monday to Friday 

• Remote working: No travel, work from home

• 13th month Bonus/December Bonus: 1 month salary as a bonus paid in December 

• Public Holidays Day off or Double Pay: - 7 paid days off per year or choose to work these for double pay

• Annual Leave/Paid time off: Additional 5 sick/vacation/annual leave days off per year

• Salary Scales model: Guaranteed salary increases when you hit certain milestones in the role as you progress & learn

• Automated workflow process with description of tasks you need to complete, checklists & videos is all built and ready for you to study, learn & follow. 

• Supportive Culture: Dedicated senior member supporting, providing feedback and answering questions

• Good career opportunity & recognition to progress 

• Small friendly team/ Family environment 



Please only apply if you have a minimum of 18 months+ experience as a loan writer | credit analyst for a New Zealand or Australian based mortgage broker, this role is not for VA’s who are only doing mortgage support tasks (such as submitting of completed deals, organising/naming client documents) this is a credit analyst role, you must have experience researching lender criteria and packaging up/ producing a lender proposal/diary note and servicing calculators. 



Please apply if you meet the criteria above and are detail orientated, have strong sense of pride/checking work, are able to focus and are committed to learning more.  



To submit your application, in strict confidence, please apply online using the appropriate link below. Your interest will be treated in the strictest of confidence.



How to apply for this role:

Click the ‘Apply now button’

Answer some basic questions: 



• What role are you applying for? 

• What is your current role, length of time & responsibilities?

• Please explain your experience/current role in how it relates to the criteria for this position which is a prerequisite of this role. 

• Why are you looking for a new role? 

• Are you available to start full time straight away? When can you start? 

• Do you have a degree or qualification? Name the qualification & what field is this in? 

• We are looking for someone who is smart, committed to learning/ improving and can carefully check their work for accuracy as is a adaptable. Why do you think you would be good for this role?



Send your Resume to 
 with the subject line job title

title: Credit Repair Specialist – Rapid Negative Item Removal
description:
Credit Repair Specialist – Rapid Negative Item Removal



We are seeking a highly skilled individual or team specializing in removing collections, charge-offs, and late payments from clients' credit reports within seven days or less. The primary strategy will involve using human trafficking letters, supplemented by supporting documentation to prevent stall letters from credit bureaus. If using data breach letters, you must also provide the necessary supporting documents to ensure successful removals.



Our CRM system is Client Dispute Manager (CDM), where client data is securely stored. If you cannot guarantee fast, effective results or provide proof of past success, we kindly ask that you do not apply. We are seeking only top-tier professionals with a proven track record in credit repair.



Key Responsibilities:

Credit Sweeps & Disputes:



Execute TransUnion credit sweeps

Perform 4-day block and removal sweeps

Dispute data breaches on Experian and Equifax reports

Use the Factual Dispute Method: Submit dispute letters via Letter Stream, the Experian website, and file complaints with CFPB/FTC

Customer Service & Communication:



Send monthly credit report updates and address client concerns via GoHighLevel and
 support

Software Proficiency:



Work efficiently with Dispute Fox, Client Dispute Manager (CDM), and Credit Repair Cloud (CRC)

Report Management:



Pull reports from IdentityIQ, MyFreeScoreNow, Smart Credit, Privacy Guard, MyScoreIQ, Credit Hero Score, and 3Score

Attorney General & BBB Disputes:



Submit disputes through the Attorney General’s office and the Better Business Bureau

Requirements:

Proven experience in credit repair with verifiable results

Familiarity with Credit Repair Cloud, Client Dispute Manager (CDM), and Dispute Fox (preferred)

High-speed internet and a reliable laptop

Strong communication and organizational skills

Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy

Compensation:

Raises and performance-based bonuses for each successfully removed negative item

Competitive pay for top performers

Application Questions:

How long have you been working in credit repair?

What is your approach to handling inquiry removal?

What credit repair strategies are you experienced with?

Are you proficient in the 24-hour Inquiry Removal Method?

If you have additional skills or achievements that make you a great fit for this role, please share them in your application. We look forward to working with elite professionals who deliver fast and effective results!

title: Care Coordinator Specialist (Pediatric Therapy)
description:
We’re looking for a reliable, detail-oriented **Care Coordinator Specialist** to support operations inside a growing pediatric therapy clinic.



This organization provides speech, occupational, and developmental therapy services for children through individualized treatment plans and family-centered care. Operating within the pediatric outpatient healthcare space, the clinic focuses on early intervention and evidence-based therapy to help children reach meaningful developmental milestones.



This role sits at the intersection of families, providers, and payers. You will manage prior authorizations, coordinate care logistics, and ensure compliance processes are handled accurately. We are looking for someone who understands healthcare systems, can operate independently, and communicates with clarity and professionalism.



This is a high-trust position that requires sound judgment, organization, and comfort handling protected health information.



---



## Why You’ll Want to Join



- You will be paid in **USD** (bi-monthly: every 15th and 30th)

- Up to **14 days** of Paid Time Off annually (starting Day 1)

- Observance of **Holidays** per company guidelines

- **100% remote setup** so you can work wherever you’re most productive

- Direct impact on improving care access for children and families

- Structured but fast-moving healthcare environment

- High-ownership role with operational visibility



---



## What You’ll Work On



### Prior Authorizations and Insurance Coordination



- Submit, track, and follow up on prior authorizations independently

- Communicate with insurance providers to resolve denials or documentation requests

- Maintain accurate records of authorization status and approvals

- Ensure timely updates to families and internal providers



### Patient and Provider Communication



- Serve as a point of contact for families regarding scheduling and insurance updates

- Coordinate with therapists to ensure services align with authorization limits

- Communicate clearly via phone, SMS, and
 with professionalism

- Represent the clinic with empathy and structure in all interactions



### Compliance and Documentation



- Handle protected health information in strict accordance with HIPAA

- Maintain organized documentation of care coordination activities

- Ensure payer requirements and documentation standards are met

- Support internal audits or compliance reviews when needed



### Credentialing and Payer Enrollment Support



- Assist with therapist license verification and tracking requirements

- Support credentialing processes and payer enrollment documentation

- Monitor renewal timelines and maintain accurate provider records

- Coordinate with leadership on compliance-related operational updates



---



## What You Bring



- **3+ years** of experience in healthcare or health tech environments such as medical practices, MSOs, or care coordination teams

- Direct experience submitting and managing prior authorizations independently

- Strong understanding of HIPAA and proper handling of PHI

- Experience communicating with patients and healthcare providers

- Working knowledge of therapist licensing requirements, credentialing, and payer enrollment

- Clear, fluent English both spoken and written

- Ability to operate independently with sound judgment

- Professional presence when representing the business externally



**Nice to Have**



- Experience in pediatric therapy or outpatient rehabilitation settings

- Familiarity with EHR or practice management systems

- Exposure to revenue cycle management processes

- Experience working in remote healthcare operations



---



## How to Apply



Please include:



1. Your **updated resume**

2. A **short 1–2 minute Loom video** introducing yourself and describing your experience managing prior authorizations and coordinating care between patients, providers, and insurance companies



**Only complete applications with a Loom video will be considered.**



---



If you are structured, dependable, and comfortable navigating the operational side of healthcare with professionalism and care, this role offers stability, responsibility, and meaningful impact in pediatric therapy services.
